fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong analyst estimates are tempered by a high forward P/E, significant debt, and low profitability margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates are very strong for the current quarter (286.1%) and fiscal year (179.5%).
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by a substantial 242.4%.
Strong liquidity with current ratio 5.03 and quick ratio 3.98.
negatives: Forward P/E of 73.43 and PEG ratio of 1.39 suggest a high valuation.
Profit margin is very low at 0.2%, and return on equity is only 1.3%.
Debt/Equity ratio is high at 311.48, with total debt exceeding total cash.
Analyst mean target of $245.13 is below the current spot price, indicating potential downside risk.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy Corporation (BE) has seen significant positive momentum driven by its role in providing power solutions for the AI revolution, including a recent fuel cell deal that reshapes AI power dynamics. The stock surged following news of Nvidia GPU price increases, which benefited AI infrastructure companies like Bloom Energy, CoreWeave, and IREN. Despite the rally, one report suggests the stock may have already surged too far, indicating potential for increased volatility.

headlines.title: This Star Hedge Fund Is Going All‑In on AI Plumbing Behind the Scenes of the Revolution
